key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep learning model can predict laser welding penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology directly from weld-pool images plus process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ weld quality monitoring and faster quality control decisions for laser penetration welding systems without relying solely on destructive inspection.
method: Uses a CMOS camera to capture top weld-pool images during welding.
Combines convolutional neural networks with state-space models to extract spatial-temporal features.
Trains a multi-task framework with welding parameters and a carefully constructed dataset for robustness.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% weld cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on camera quality, weld-process consistency, and the representativeness of the constructed dataset when deployed on new welding setups.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
